observations.summary: The review-tests step flagged 3 skipped Story 5.1 E2E test blocks with 'Recommendation: Remove' (each covered by component tests, one with an empty body) but did not remove them — the prompt says 'flag' for skipped tests unlike 'fix directly' for stale markers and empty stubs, so the skipped tests persist and will recur as noise on future runs.

Report line 196: 'no direct action was taken since they have legitimate skip reasons with component-test coverage documented.' amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). When the skip reason is no longer applicable — the behavior is covered by other tests, the environmental dependency is permanent with no planned fix, or the test body is empty — remove the skipped test directly rather than only flagging it, since a skipped test that will never be un-skipped is a transitional artifact that inflates the count without verifying behavior. Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation. Treat empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions, only a comment or empty body) as transitional artifacts — remove them directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories, since an empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.
amendments.rationale: The review-tests step has flagged but not removed skipped tests from prior stories in 3 stories (3.3, 3.4, 5.2), causing them to persist and recur as noise on every future run; the prompt says 'flag' for skipped tests unlike 'fix directly' for stale markers and empty stubs, so the agent recommends removal but does not act on its own recommendation.
